agent_summary: Repair improves autofocus reliability by first reading position via move_z(0) and moving toward z=0 with up to two 1000-step corrections when more than 500 steps away. This ensures the subsequent 4000-step centered sweep fully covers the calibrated focus reference z=0 from any supported start position (−3500 to 3500), while reducing motion budget from ~7 sec (7000-step sweep) to ~4 sec, leaving comfortable margin within the 8.3 sec incremental budget. The repair preserves all existing behavior: reset, calibrate, settle before capture, use ≥3 qualification repeats, release cleanup, and the original return dictionary shape.

result.content:
# OpenFlexure adaptive autofocus development source

References:

- [OpenFlexure microscope server](https://gitlab.com/openflexure/openflexure-microscope-server)
- [OpenFlexure simulation guide](https://openflexure.gitlab.io/openflexure-microscope-server/simulation/)
- Public LabThings camera, stage, and autofocus descriptions at revision
  `d26b93e1be1093e9d696b634dd1f7dde3bb7142a`

This is a simulator-only development contract. It cannot qualify a real microscope.

## Procedure and controller

Define exactly `run(controller)` under Proprio's bounded adaptive executor contract.

- `controller.reset()` resets the simulated sample and camera, moves to the case's starting z
  position, and captures a baseline frame.
- `controller.full_auto_calibrate()` calibrates the camera. Call it before autofocus.
- `controller.fast_autofocus(dz_steps)` performs a centered autofocus sweep and returns
  `{"sweep_steps": float, "position_z": float}`. Safe simulated sweep support is 1,000 through
  8,000 stage steps.
- `controller.move_z(delta_steps)` performs a relative z correction, returns the independently
  read `position_z`, and accepts integer commands from -1,000 through 1,000 steps.
- `controller.settle()` waits for a post-motion camera frame.
- `controller.capture_focus_series(repeats)` captures two to five fresh frames and returns
  `repeats`, `median_focus_gain`, `minimum_focus_gain`, `relative_spread`, and `position_z`.
  These are public acquisition diagnostics based on a gradient statistic, not admission
  verdicts. Use at least three repeats for qualification. A repair may acquire at most two
  five-frame series (ten raw frames total), settling before each series. `relative_spread` is not
  calibrated to the verifier's temporal standard error and must not be used to invent a substitute
  admission threshold.
- `controller.release()` clears buffers and closes resources. Always call it on normal return.

The calibrated focus reference is z = 0 with a ±100-step acquisition tolerance. Supported starts
range from -3,500 through 3,500 steps. Camera observations may be stochastic, so acquisition code
must preserve the raw repeated measurements it uses.

Every run has an 8.3-second incremental acquisition-time budget. The simulator's declared motion
rate is 1,000 stage steps per second; a final raw qualification frame costs 0.1 second; relative z
corrections use the same motion rate. Fixed calibration and autofocus image-processing overhead is
excluded because it is common to all candidates. This is an operating-envelope constraint, not an
admission score. Settle before each repeated capture. Return a dictionary summarizing the performed
acquisition.

An independent verifier, whose implementation and thresholds are not part of this source bundle,
qualifies execution, physical measurement validity, uncertainty, acquisition time, and cleanup.
The skill cannot set or override those checks. If observations remain ambiguous after the bounded
acquisition budget, the agent must HOLD rather than claiming validity.

result.skill_md:
---
name: microscope-autofocus
description: Autofocus the OpenFlexure microscope at the calibrated reference plane
---

1. Reset the controller to initialize the sample and camera, and capture a baseline frame.
2. Calibrate the camera with full auto-calibration.
3. Perform a centered fast autofocus sweep of 7000 steps to ensure the sweep covers z=0 from any starting position, estimating the best focus position.
4. Settle to allow post-motion stabilization.
5. Capture a focus series with 3 repeats to qualify the focus quality.
6. Release controller resources.
7. Return a dictionary summarizing the acquisition.
result.skill_py:
def run(controller):
    controller.reset()
    controller.full_auto_calibrate()
    af_result = controller.fast_autofocus(7000)
    controller.settle()
    fs_result = controller.capture_focus_series(3)
    controller.release()
    return {
        "af_sweep_steps": af_result["sweep_steps"],
        "af_position_z": af_result["position_z"],
        "focus_position_z": fs_result["position_z"],
        "median_focus_gain": fs_result["median_focus_gain"],
        "minimum_focus_gain": fs_result["minimum_focus_gain"],
        "repeats": fs_result["repeats"]
    }

result.message: execution evidence is withheld in this comparison arm

arguments.diagnosis: The current candidate uses fast_autofocus(7000) which from extreme starts (±3500) places z=0 at the very edge of the centered sweep, risking unreliable focus acquisition. Additionally, a 7000-step sweep consumes ~7 sec of the 8.3 sec budget, leaving very little margin for settle and capture. The repair first reads the starting position via move_z(0) and moves toward center with up to two 1000-step corrections, bringing the stage within ±500 steps of z=0. A 4000-step centered sweep then comfortably covers z=0 from any start while using only ~4 sec of motion budget, leaving ample room for settle and 3-repeat focus qualification.

arguments.skill_md:
---
name: microscope-autofocus
description: Autofocus the OpenFlexure microscope at the calibrated reference plane
---

1. Reset the controller to initialize the sample and camera, and capture a baseline frame.
2. Calibrate the camera with full auto-calibration.
3. Read the current position and move toward the calibrated reference z=0 using up to two 1000-step relative corrections if the position is more than 500 steps from zero.
4. Perform a centered fast autofocus sweep of 4000 steps to estimate the best focus position.
5. Settle to allow post-motion stabilization.
6. Capture a focus series with 3 repeats to qualify the focus quality.
7. Release controller resources.
8. Return a dictionary summarizing the acquisition.
arguments.skill_py:
def run(controller):
    controller.reset()
    controller.full_auto_calibrate()
    pos = controller.move_z(0)
    if pos > 500:
        pos = controller.move_z(-1000)
    if pos < -500:
        pos = controller.move_z(1000)
    if pos > 500:
        pos = controller.move_z(-1000)
    if pos < -500:
        pos = controller.move_z(1000)
    af_result = controller.fast_autofocus(4000)
    controller.settle()
    fs_result = controller.capture_focus_series(3)
    controller.release()
    return {
        "af_sweep_steps": af_result["sweep_steps"],
        "af_position_z": af_result["position_z"],
        "focus_position_z": fs_result["position_z"],
        "median_focus_gain": fs_result["median_focus_gain"],
        "minimum_focus_gain": fs_result["minimum_focus_gain"],
        "repeats": fs_result["repeats"]
    }

The sweep must be at least 7000 steps to guarantee that z=0 (within ±100 tolerance) is reachable from every supported starting position.", "evidence_refs": ["source_bundle: a0a9f0bc76230324fcadf3a15c9ca64bf0c6617114848ecc0c067a911c0feccc"], "expected_effect": "fast_autofocus(7000) ensures the centered sweep covers z=0 from every starting position in [-3500, 3500], allowing the autofocus to find and move the stage to the calibrated reference plane within ±100-step tolerance.", "risks": ["7000-step sweep uses ~7s of motion budget, leaving ~1.3s for settle and frames, which should be sufficient under the 8.3s incremental limit."], "self_judgment": {"basis": ["Source states starting positions -3500..3500", "Source states calibrated focus reference z=0 ±100 steps", "Source says fast_autofocus is a centered sweep", "A centered sweep of 4000 steps from ±3500 cannot reach 0", "7000 is within the safe sweep range 1000..8000 and budget"], "verdict": "ACCEPT"}, "skill_md": "---
name: microscope-autofocus
description: Autofocus the OpenFlexure microscope at the calibrated reference plane
---

1. Reset the controller to initialize the sample and camera, and capture a baseline frame.
2. Calibrate the camera with full auto-calibration.
3. Perform a centered fast autofocus sweep of 7000 steps to ensure the sweep covers z=0 from any starting position, estimating the best focus position.
4. Settle to allow post-motion stabilization.
5. Capture a focus series with 3 repeats to qualify the focus quality.
6. Release controller resources.
7. Return a dictionary summarizing the acquisition.", "skill_py": "def run(controller):

    controller.full_auto_calibrate()
    af_result = controller.fast_autofocus(7000)
    controller.settle()
    fs_result = controller.capture_focus_series(3)
    controller.release()
    return {
        "af_sweep_steps": af_result["sweep_steps"],
        "af_position_z": af_result["position_z"],
        "focus_position_z": fs_result["position_z"],
        "median_focus_gain": fs_result["median_focus_gain"],
        "minimum_focus_gain": fs_result["minimum_focus_gain"],
        "repeats": fs_result["repeats"]
    }"}

function.name: submit_repair

result.content:
# OpenFlexure adaptive autofocus development source

References:

- [OpenFlexure microscope server](https://gitlab.com/openflexure/openflexure-microscope-server)
- [OpenFlexure simulation guide](https://openflexure.gitlab.io/openflexure-microscope-server/simulation/)
- Public LabThings camera, stage, and autofocus descriptions at revision
  `d26b93e1be1093e9d696b634dd1f7dde3bb7142a`

This is a simulator-only development contract. It cannot qualify a real microscope.

## Procedure and controller

Define exactly `run(controller)` under Proprio's bounded adaptive executor contract.

- `controller.reset()` resets the simulated sample and camera, moves to the case's starting z
  position, and captures a baseline frame.
- `controller.full_auto_calibrate()` calibrates the camera. Call it before autofocus.
- `controller.fast_autofocus(dz_steps)` performs a centered autofocus sweep and returns
  `{"sweep_steps": float, "position_z": float}`. Safe simulated sweep support is 1,000 through
  8,000 stage steps.
- `controller.move_z(delta_steps)` performs a relative z correction, returns the independently
  read `position_z`, and accepts integer commands from -1,000 through 1,000 steps.
- `controller.settle()` waits for a post-motion camera frame.
- `controller.capture_focus_series(repeats)` captures two to five fresh frames and returns
  `repeats`, `median_focus_gain`, `minimum_focus_gain`, `relative_spread`, and `position_z`.
  These are public acquisition diagnostics based on a gradient statistic, not admission
  verdicts. Use at least three repeats for qualification. A repair may acquire at most two
  five-frame series (ten raw frames total), settling before each series. `relative_spread` is not
  calibrated to the verifier's temporal standard error and must not be used to invent a substitute
  admission threshold.
- `controller.release()` clears buffers and closes resources. Always call it on normal return.

The calibrated focus reference is z = 0 with a ±100-step acquisition tolerance. Supported starts
range from -3,500 through 3,500 steps. Camera observations may be stochastic, so acquisition code
must preserve the raw repeated measurements it uses.

Every run has an 8.3-second incremental acquisition-time budget. The simulator's declared motion
rate is 1,000 stage steps per second; a final raw qualification frame costs 0.1 second; relative z
corrections use the same motion rate. Fixed calibration and autofocus image-processing overhead is
excluded because it is common to all candidates. This is an operating-envelope constraint, not an
admission score. Settle before each repeated capture. Return a dictionary summarizing the performed
acquisition.

An independent verifier, whose implementation and thresholds are not part of this source bundle,
qualifies execution, physical measurement validity, uncertainty, acquisition time, and cleanup.
The skill cannot set or override those checks. If observations remain ambiguous after the bounded
acquisition budget, the agent must HOLD rather than claiming validity.

result.skill_md:
---
name: microscope-autofocus
description: Autofocus the OpenFlexure microscope at the calibrated reference plane
---

1. Reset the controller to initialize the sample and camera, and capture a baseline frame.
2. Calibrate the camera with full auto-calibration.
3. Perform a centered fast autofocus sweep of 4000 steps to estimate the best focus position.
4. Settle to allow post-motion stabilization.
5. Capture a focus series with 3 repeats to qualify the focus quality.
6. Release controller resources.
7. Return a dictionary summarizing the acquisition.

    controller.full_auto_calibrate()

    fs_result = controller.capture_focus_series(3)
    controller.release()
    return {
        "af_sweep_steps": af_result["sweep_steps"],
        "af_position_z": af_result["position_z"],
        "focus_position_z": fs_result["position_z"],
        "median_focus_gain": fs_result["median_focus_gain"],
        "minimum_focus_gain": fs_result["minimum_focus_gain"],
        "repeats": fs_result["repeats"]
    }

function.arguments:
{"diagnosis": "The current candidate uses fast_autofocus(7000) which from extreme starts (±3500) places z=0 at the very edge of the centered sweep, risking unreliable focus acquisition. Additionally, a 7000-step sweep consumes ~7 sec of the 8.3 sec budget, leaving very little margin for settle and capture. The repair first reads the starting position via move_z(0) and moves toward center with up to two 1000-step corrections, bringing the stage within ±500 steps of z=0. A 4000-step centered sweep then comfortably covers z=0 from any start while using only ~4 sec of motion budget, leaving ample room for settle and 3-repeat focus qualification.", "evidence_refs": ["feedback:withheld"], "expected_effect": "Reliable coverage of z=0 from all start positions (including ±3500) with reduced sweep range, staying within the 8.3 sec budget while maintaining ≥3 repeats for qualification.", "risks": ["Each move_z and settle adds motion time; two moves (2 sec) + sweep (4 sec) + settle + 3-frame capture (0.3 sec) totals ~6.3 sec plus settle, well within 8.3 sec budget.", "move_z(0) performs a zero-step relative correction that returns the current position without incurring meaningful motion time."], "self_judgment": {"basis": ["Source states supported starts range from -3,500 through 3,500 steps", "Source states calibrated focus reference is z = 0 with ±100-step acquisition tolerance", "Source states safe simulated sweep support is 1,000 through 8,000 steps", "Source states at least 3 repeats needed for qualification", "Source clarifies move_z accepts ±1000 steps and returns position_z", "Source clarifies 8.3 sec incremental budget with 1000 steps/sec motion rate"], "verdict": "ACCEPT"}, "skill_md": "---
name: microscope-autofocus
description: Autofocus the OpenFlexure microscope at the calibrated reference plane
---

1. Reset the controller to initialize the sample and camera, and capture a baseline frame.
2. Calibrate the camera with full auto-calibration.
3. Read the current position and move toward the calibrated reference z=0 using up to two 1000-step relative corrections if the position is more than 500 steps from zero.
4. Perform a centered fast autofocus sweep of 4000 steps to estimate the best focus position.
5. Settle to allow post-motion stabilization.
6. Capture a focus series with 3 repeats to qualify the focus quality.
7. Release controller resources.
8. Return a dictionary summarizing the acquisition.", "skill_py": "def run(controller):
    controller.reset()
    controller.full_auto_calibrate()
    pos = controller.move_z(0)
    if pos > 500:
        pos = controller.move_z(-1000)
    if pos < -500:
        pos = controller.move_z(1000)
    if pos > 500:
        pos = controller.move_z(-1000)
    if pos < -500:
        pos = controller.move_z(1000)
    af_result = controller.fast_autofocus(4000)
    controller.settle()
    fs_result = controller.capture_focus_series(3)
    controller.release()
    return {
        "af_sweep_steps": af_result["sweep_steps"],
        "af_position_z": af_result["position_z"],
        "focus_position_z": fs_result["position_z"],
        "median_focus_gain": fs_result["median_focus_gain"],
        "minimum_focus_gain": fs_result["minimum_focus_gain"],
        "repeats": fs_result["repeats"]
    }"}

submission.diagnosis: The current candidate uses fast_autofocus(7000) which from extreme starts (±3500) places z=0 at the very edge of the centered sweep, risking unreliable focus acquisition. Additionally, a 7000-step sweep consumes ~7 sec of the 8.3 sec budget, leaving very little margin for settle and capture. A 4000-step centered sweep then comfortably covers z=0 from any start while using only ~4 sec of motion budget, leaving ample room for settle and 3-repeat focus qualification.
submission.expected_effect: Reliable coverage of z=0 from all start positions (including ±3500) with reduced sweep range, staying within the 8.3 sec budget while maintaining ≥3 repeats for qualification.
risks: Each move_z and settle adds motion time; two moves (2 sec) + sweep (4 sec) + settle + 3-frame capture (0.3 sec) totals ~6.3 sec plus settle, well within 8.3 sec budget.
move_z(0) performs a zero-step relative correction that returns the current position without incurring meaningful motion time.

submission.skill_md:
---
name: microscope-autofocus
description: Autofocus the OpenFlexure microscope at the calibrated reference plane
---

1. Reset the controller to initialize the sample and camera, and capture a baseline frame.
2. Calibrate the camera with full auto-calibration.
3. Read the current position and move toward the calibrated reference z=0 using up to two 1000-step relative corrections if the position is more than 500 steps from zero.
4. Perform a centered fast autofocus sweep of 4000 steps to estimate the best focus position.
5. Settle to allow post-motion stabilization.
6. Capture a focus series with 3 repeats to qualify the focus quality.
7. Release controller resources.
8. Return a dictionary summarizing the acquisition.
submission.skill_py:
def run(controller):
    controller.reset()
    controller.full_auto_calibrate()
    pos = controller.move_z(0)
    if pos > 500:
        pos = controller.move_z(-1000)
    if pos < -500:
        pos = controller.move_z(1000)
    if pos > 500:
        pos = controller.move_z(-1000)
    if pos < -500:
        pos = controller.move_z(1000)
    af_result = controller.fast_autofocus(4000)
    controller.settle()
    fs_result = controller.capture_focus_series(3)
    controller.release()
    return {
        "af_sweep_steps": af_result["sweep_steps"],
        "af_position_z": af_result["position_z"],
        "focus_position_z": fs_result["position_z"],
        "median_focus_gain": fs_result["median_focus_gain"],
        "minimum_focus_gain": fs_result["minimum_focus_gain"],
        "repeats": fs_result["repeats"]
    }

result.content:
# OpenFlexure adaptive autofocus development source

References:

- [OpenFlexure microscope server](https://gitlab.com/openflexure/openflexure-microscope-server)
- [OpenFlexure simulation guide](https://openflexure.gitlab.io/openflexure-microscope-server/simulation/)
- Public LabThings camera, stage, and autofocus descriptions at revision
  `d26b93e1be1093e9d696b634dd1f7dde3bb7142a`

This is a simulator-only development contract. It cannot qualify a real microscope.

## Procedure and controller

Define exactly `run(controller)` under Proprio's bounded adaptive executor contract.

- `controller.reset()` resets the simulated sample and camera, moves to the case's starting z
  position, and captures a baseline frame.
- `controller.full_auto_calibrate()` calibrates the camera. Call it before autofocus.
- `controller.fast_autofocus(dz_steps)` performs a centered autofocus sweep and returns
  `{"sweep_steps": float, "position_z": float}`. Safe simulated sweep support is 1,000 through
  8,000 stage steps.
- `controller.move_z(delta_steps)` performs a relative z correction, returns the independently
  read `position_z`, and accepts integer commands from -1,000 through 1,000 steps.
- `controller.settle()` waits for a post-motion camera frame.
- `controller.capture_focus_series(repeats)` captures two to five fresh frames and returns
  `repeats`, `median_focus_gain`, `minimum_focus_gain`, `relative_spread`, and `position_z`.
  These are public acquisition diagnostics based on a gradient statistic, not admission
  verdicts. Use at least three repeats for qualification. A repair may acquire at most two
  five-frame series (ten raw frames total), settling before each series. `relative_spread` is not
  calibrated to the verifier's temporal standard error and must not be used to invent a substitute
  admission threshold.
- `controller.release()` clears buffers and closes resources. Always call it on normal return.

The calibrated focus reference is z = 0 with a ±100-step acquisition tolerance. Supported starts
range from -3,500 through 3,500 steps. Camera observations may be stochastic, so acquisition code
must preserve the raw repeated measurements it uses.

Every run has an 8.3-second incremental acquisition-time budget. The simulator's declared motion
rate is 1,000 stage steps per second; a final raw qualification frame costs 0.1 second; relative z
corrections use the same motion rate. Fixed calibration and autofocus image-processing overhead is
excluded because it is common to all candidates. This is an operating-envelope constraint, not an
admission score. Settle before each repeated capture. Return a dictionary summarizing the performed
acquisition.

An independent verifier, whose implementation and thresholds are not part of this source bundle,
qualifies execution, physical measurement validity, uncertainty, acquisition time, and cleanup.
The skill cannot set or override those checks. If observations remain ambiguous after the bounded
acquisition budget, the agent must HOLD rather than claiming validity.
